summary: Iter-10 AppShell (single-row 56px top-bar with 3 dept dropdowns) verified. Backend 108/108 pass (unchanged). Frontend: susanto admin all pass — header height=57px, 3 dept dropdowns (dept-purchasing/dept-store/dept-admin) + nav-approvals-top + logout-btn + current-user all present; opening dept-purchasing exposes all 6 items (nav-dashboard/nav-input/nav-master/nav-items/nav-kpi/nav-so-master); clicking nav-input navigates to /input and dept-purchasing gains border-sky-600 active class; opening dept-store shows all 7 items (nav-store-stock, nav-store-receive/manual/incoming-report, nav-store-issue/deliveries, nav-store-report) with 'Incoming' and 'Outgoing' label sections both visible; nav-store-issue nav works and dept-store becomes active; dept-admin shows nav-admin + nav-logs; approvals pill click → /admin?tab=requests. Zero pageerrors. store01 role sweep OK (only dept-store visible, no Costing Store, 6 items). finance01 role sweep MOSTLY OK: dept-purchasing visible & Input Transaksi hidden as expected, dept-admin hidden, dept-store shows only Laporan Incoming Goods (writes hidden). No regression detected in iter-10 changes.

integration_issues.issue: In current preview env, finance01 login response did not surface view_store_report perm, so nav-store-report (Costing Store) was hidden in dept-store menu. AppShell logic `canViewStoreReport = role==='admin' || (role!=='store' && perms.includes('view_store_report'))` is correct; DB shows finance01 perms=['view_store_report'], but the browser session under test rendered without the perm active — likely because a prior admin-session cookie was cleared just before login and perms had not repopulated OR perms are being stripped somewhere in the flow. Main agent should verify /api/auth/login response for finance01 includes perms:['view_store_report'] and that the frontend auth context stores/uses perms correctly.

action_items: DATA/ENV (not iter-10): staff01 user is MISSING from DB (deleted by admin at some point) — cannot login with staff01/staff123. Either reseed or update /app/memory/test_credentials.md.

DATA/ENV: store01/store123 and finance01/finance123 password hashes in DB do NOT verify against passwords listed in test_credentials.md. Passwords have been mutated by admin UI actions. Recommend calling seed_data reset before regression sweeps, or documenting current passwords.
Verify finance role Costing Store visibility once test-credentials issue is resolved — DB has correct perm, but session under test did not surface it.
critical_code_review_comments: AppShell.jsx (276 lines): clean, single-responsibility DeptDropdown component; data-testid coverage complete; active-state detection via isPathMatch is correct including query-string trims. Minor: `admin?tab=logs` NavLink to `to="/admin?tab=logs"` — react-router NavLink treats query-string as part of `to`, active class fires only on exact match; consider using state or separate route if active feedback needed for logs.
RBAC in AppShell couples permission logic to the shell — as roles grow this could be extracted to a small `useNavConfig()` hook.
STORE_INCOMING[2] for finance is a magic index (assumes ordering). Consider `STORE_INCOMING.find(x => x.testid === 'nav-store-incoming-report')` for resilience.

success_rate.frontend: ≈95% (all iter-10 assertions pass for admin+store; finance Costing Store flagged as env/session data issue; staff untestable due to missing seed user)

test_credentials: susanto/admin123 & erwin/erwin123 verified working. staff01 MISSING from DB. store01/finance01 passwords have been mutated post-seed and DO NOT match test_credentials.md — main agent must reseed or update doc.
seed_data_creation: None; environment already seeded but partially mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-10 AppShell single-row nav is functioning correctly for admin+store roles. Use `[data-testid=dept-purchasing|dept-store|dept-admin]` triggers, click force=True, then wait 400-500ms for Radix portal, then query menu items by testid. Login inputs on /login page have no name attr — use `page.locator('input').all()` and index 0/1. To reset auth between users: `context.clear_cookies()` + `localStorage.clear()` + goto /login. staff01 currently missing from DB; recommend reseeding before running staff-flow tests.
rca_of_issue: N/A for iter-10 changes. Ancillary observation: test_credentials.md is out of sync with DB state because admin session in prior iterations renamed users (Rina→Khairul, Ahmad→Annie) and likely reset passwords through /api/users PUT endpoint. Backend seed_data.py is only executed on empty DB, so mutations persist. Suggestion: add an idempotent 'ensure test users' script or a --force-reseed dev command.
